Vol. 02 · MMXXVI
Edition № 02 — Selected Works Open Read · Always

Ehsan.

Siddiqui

Content strategist &
copywriter — building
narratives that land.

A blend of creativity and strategic thinking shaped by a rich background in marketing and SEO. The work isn't only about crafting compelling narratives — it's about driving results and engaging audiences. Insightful articles, persuasive web copy, social-first creatives, and integrated brand campaigns that resonate across very different demographics.

Practice · Point of View

Good copy doesn't shout. It listens first.

Across a plethora of published articles, a plethora of brand creatives, and live integrated campaigns spanning healthcare, environment, sport, lifestyle and home, the through-line stays the same: understand the reader before writing for them.

That shows up as headlines tuned for search but not engineered to death. Visual creatives that earn the scroll-stop. Short-form scripts that earn the second second. Long-form features that hold attention past the fold. Decks that move clients past the polite nod into the actual decision.

Strategy without craft is a brief. Craft without strategy is a pretty paragraph nobody reads. The work below tries to keep both in the same room.

Section I · Visual Work

Creatives, on brand.

Concept, copy and creative direction across a plethora of brand campaigns — healthcare, environmental advocacy, lifestyle, sport and home. Selected campaigns below.

Campaign № 01

Saroj Hospitals — mental health awareness

Healthcare
Social · Awareness

A series built to make the unspoken speakable. Direct headlines, considered imagery, and a hashtag — #LetsTalk — designed to invite, not instruct.

Campaign № 02

Hawa Badlo (GAIL India) — environmental awareness

Public Sector · CSR
Social · #TimeToActNow

A national awareness series for GAIL India under the Hawa Badlo banner — built around the idea that air, water and waste are not abstract concerns but daily decisions.

Campaign № 03

Little Nap Recliners — brand storytelling

D2C · Lifestyle
Social · Editorial Concept

Three threads under one brand: a Men's Day campaign in Hinglish that flips the usual script on masculinity, a Zero Discrimination Day post that says less and means more, and an editorial-style "Little Nap Times" concept that treats comfort like breaking news.

Campaign № 04

Bhutan Tuff — cricket meets construction

Building Materials · B2C
ICC World Cup tie-in

Plywood is not the easiest sell on the feed. Tying the brand language to the cricketing calendar — cover drives, perfect catches, technique — gave a functional product the cultural pull it needed.

Campaign № 05

Yokohama Tyres — Lifetime Protection Program

Automotive
Service Launch

A reassurance-led launch creative for a tyre warranty program. The promise is technical; the writing keeps it human.

Campaign № 06

Victor Badminton — Ready To Win

Sport · Performance Apparel
Athlete-led campaign

Performance-first lines for a performance brand. Each post written to sit beside the athlete, not in front of them — short, kinetic, earned.
